description.task: Create a DockerfileAnalyzer class that parses a simplified Dockerfile (as a list of instruction strings) and analyzes its layer structure. Count cacheable layers, detect security issues (running as root, latest tag), and suggest optimizations.
instructions: layerCount() counts instructions whose command is FROM, RUN, COPY, or ADD
securityWarnings() adds 'Running as root: no USER instruction found' if no USER instruction exists
securityWarnings() adds 'Using latest tag in FROM is not recommended' if any FROM args contains ':latest' or no tag (no ':' in image name)
hasOptimalCopyOrder() returns true if there is at least one RUN instruction whose index precedes the index of the last COPY instruction
Comment lines (starting with #) and blank lines are already filtered out by the constructor
description.hint: layerCount(): filter instructions where Set.of('FROM','RUN','COPY','ADD').contains(cmd), count. hasOptimalCopyOrder(): find lastCopyIndex, find any RUN index < lastCopyIndex. securityWarnings(): check no instruction has 'USER' command; check FROM args with !args.contains(':').
description.expectedOutput:
Layers: 5
Warnings: [Running as root: no USER instruction found, Using latest tag in FROM is not recommended]
Optimal COPY order: true

description.task: Build a DockerImageCache class that simulates Docker's layer caching mechanism. Each image is built from an ordered list of layers. When layers match a prior build, the cache is reused. Implement cache hit detection and invalidation (when a layer changes, all subsequent layers are invalidated).
instructions: cacheBuild() stores a copy of the layers list under the image tag
cacheHitLayers() compares newLayers against cached layers element by element from index 0
Count consecutive matches until the first mismatch or the end of either list
layersToRebuild() returns newLayers sublist starting from the first mismatch index
Return 0 and full list respectively if the image tag is not in the cache
description.hint: In cacheHitLayers(): get cached list, iterate with index, stop when layers differ. Return the count. In layersToRebuild(): use cacheHitLayers() to get the hit count, then return newLayers.subList(hitCount, newLayers.size()).

description.task: Implement a ContainerResourceValidator class that validates Docker container resource configurations against platform constraints. Check memory limits, CPU quotas, and port bindings to ensure containers are configured safely for production deployment.
instructions: Memory error: add 'Memory limit too low: minimum 128MB' when memoryLimitMb < 128
CPU error: add 'CPU limit out of range: 0.1 to 8.0' when cpuLimit <= 0 or cpuLimit > 8.0
Port error: for each port < 1024, add 'Privileged port <N> requires root'
Memory warning: add 'Consider increasing memory limit for production' when memoryLimitMb < 256
ReadOnlyRootFs warning: add 'Consider enabling read-only root filesystem' when readOnlyRootFs is false
description.hint: For privileged ports, iterate exposedPorts: if (port < 1024) errors.add('Privileged port ' + port + ' requires root'). Memory checks: error at < 128, warning at < 256 (after the error check).
description.expectedOutput:
Valid: false
Errors: [Memory limit too low: minimum 128MB, Privileged port 80 requires root]
Warnings: [Consider increasing memory limit for production, Consider enabling read-only root filesystem]
